_meta.provenance: Module-specific Layer-1 integration register: how each module relates to each existing system. Every integration is a Proposed design; system facts come from the verified systems register. Modules not listed here have not been authored yet (Phase 2 in progress, pillar by pillar).

integrations.why: Academies and training centres registered on NSRS map to directory nodes, so the org tree and the registry describe the same institutions.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS institution records → directory node seeding and cross-checks (one-way, periodic).
integrations.kbValueAdd: NSRS lists institutions; the directory adds hierarchy, roles, jurisdictions and routing.
integrations.gaps: NSRS does not model administrative hierarchy, sanctioned posts or jurisdictions.

integrations.why: eOffice instances already encode ministry/department role hierarchies for file routing.
integrations.dataFlow: eOffice organisational units → cross-check of directory nodes (no live sync proposed initially).
integrations.kbValueAdd: One public, sports-wide directory across bodies that live in separate eOffice instances.
integrations.gaps: eOffice hierarchies are per-instance and not publicly queryable.

integrations.why: Hosting prerequisite for the first platform registry.
integrations.dataFlow: None — infrastructure requirement.

integrations.why: The NSGA 2025 is the statutory basis: recognition, elections, tenure/cooling-off, athlete commissions, Tribunal — M2 is its digital compliance backbone.
integrations.dataFlow: Statute → compliance rule set encoded in the registry (recognition states, filing obligations).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Turns a paper statute into a live, queryable compliance scorecard.
integrations.gaps: NSB operating procedures and register formats not yet published; select provisions only in force since 31 Dec 2025.

integrations.why: NSRS registers federations/academies; recognition status must attach to the same entities.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS federation records ↔ registry entity matching (shared identifiers needed).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Recognition + compliance status on top of bare registration.
integrations.gaps: No public federation-status API on NSRS.

integrations.why: PFMS is the authoritative source for sanction → release → utilisation of central scheme money.
integrations.dataFlow: PFMS payment-stage events for sports schemes → application timeline mirror (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Athlete-visible fund tracking attached to the application, not buried in a finance portal.
integrations.gaps: No confirmed external API; state-funded grants flow outside PFMS (Q3).

integrations.why: Scheme files physically move through eOffice; applicants should see desk and days-pending.
integrations.dataFlow: eOffice file-status events (file no. ↔ application ID) → status mirror (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: “Where is my file” answered without RTI; delay analytics by desk.
integrations.gaps: No public eOffice API; mapping file numbers to applications needs ministry cooperation.

integrations.why: DigiLocker is the confirmed-API path for document submission and later certificate issuance.
integrations.dataFlow: Issued documents pulled with applicant consent; KB-issued certificates pushed as issuer (later).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Zero photocopies; forgery-resistant documents.
integrations.gaps: Requires formal partner onboarding (SOP) — not yet applied for.

integrations.why: Khelo India scholarships/TOPS stipends are the highest-volume schemes to onboard first.
integrations.dataFlow: Scheme masters + beneficiary decisions ↔ application workflow.
integrations.kbValueAdd: One application front door; eligibility engine over scheme rules.
integrations.gaps: Scheme rules exist as documents, not structured eligibility rules.

integrations.why: Benefit payouts ride DBT rails; status closes the loop for the applicant.
integrations.dataFlow: DBT payment confirmations → application timeline (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: “Money reached the account” visible next to the approval.
integrations.gaps: DBT confirmation is via PFMS; direct confirmation pending (S12 partially verified).

integrations.why: CPGRAMS is the national grievance system connected to 92 ministries and 36 states/UTs — a sports channel must interoperate, not compete.
integrations.dataFlow: Sports cases raised in KB get CPGRAMS cross-references where applicable; category mapping agreed with DARPG.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Sport-specific routing (via M1 jurisdictions), SLA clocks and validity filtering in front of the generic national system.
integrations.gaps: Whether DARPG permits a domain front-end or requires cases to live wholly in CPGRAMS is undecided (Q19).

integrations.why: DPDP applies to targeted messaging and grievance records (minors' safeguarding cases especially).
integrations.dataFlow: None — compliance constraint shaping consent and retention design.
integrations.gaps: Final DPDP Rules phase-in affects messaging-consent design.

integrations.why: Schools already run annual fitness assessments on Fit India's age-appropriate GOALS protocols — the screening games must extend this battery, not compete with it.
integrations.dataFlow: Fit India protocol definitions → screening test battery; school registration status ← cross-checked.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Turns a fitness-rating exercise into a talent-discovery funnel with verified results.
integrations.gaps: Fit India assesses fitness but does not shortlist or route talent anywhere.

integrations.why: UDISE+ gives every recognised school an 11-digit code — the stable identifier the screening network keys on.
integrations.dataFlow: UDISE+ school records → school register seeding and yearly refresh (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: No new school registry; screening coverage is measurable against the real national school universe.
integrations.gaps: UDISE+ has no sports data, facilities detail beyond basics, or screening history.

integrations.why: KIRTI's Talent Assessment Centres are the next step for children who stand out in school screening.
integrations.dataFlow: Top-percentile shortlists → KIRTI referral queue; KIRTI assessment outcomes → child's timeline.
integrations.kbValueAdd: KIRTI gets a pre-filtered, geography-complete funnel instead of self-selected registrations.
integrations.gaps: KIRTI Phase 1 assessed ~51,000 of a 20-lakh target — the funnel, not the centres, is the bottleneck.

integrations.why: APAAR is the student's lifetime education ID; linking it (with guardian consent) connects screening history to the child's education record.
integrations.dataFlow: APAAR ID ← guardian-consented linkage at enrolment; no education data pulled without purpose.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Screening results survive school transfers; dedupe across schools becomes trivial.
integrations.gaps: APAAR consent is school-mediated and withdrawable — linkage must handle revocation.

integrations.why: A screened child who continues in sport needs one identity from day one — the Khelo India ID issued by NSRS.
integrations.dataFlow: Standout athletes get NSRS registration initiated (guardian-consented); KID attaches to screening history.
integrations.kbValueAdd: The 'first scouted by' record and the elite career share one identity spine.
integrations.gaps: NSRS self-registration assumes motivated adults; child-onboarding flow does not exist publicly.

integrations.why: Teacher–school mapping rides the same UDISE+ school codes; a scout's jurisdiction is their school.
integrations.dataFlow: School codes → scout registration validation (teacher claims verified against school).
integrations.kbValueAdd: A national scout force addressable by real school geography.
integrations.gaps: UDISE+ teacher data is administrative — scout enrolment still needs education-department consent.

integrations.why: Fit India school registration and ratings already engage PT teachers — the scout programme is the recognition layer on the same network.
integrations.dataFlow: Fit India school participation status → scout-programme eligibility signals.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Teachers get named, credited and rewarded for discovery work Fit India already implies.
integrations.gaps: Fit India recognises schools, not individual teachers.

integrations.why: NSRS registers coaches and support personnel; scouts belong on the same registry so discovery credit is portable.
integrations.dataFlow: Scout registrations → NSRS support-personnel records (or linked profile).
integrations.kbValueAdd: 'First scouted by' becomes a permanent, verifiable career credit.
integrations.gaps: No public scout/talent-spotter category confirmed on NSRS.

integrations.why: NSRS is the proposed identity backbone: every record on the 360 timeline attaches to the Khelo India ID (KID).
integrations.dataFlow: Identity lookup/create ↔ NSRS; profile events (results, assessments, benefits) attach to KID.
integrations.kbValueAdd: NSRS holds registrations; the 360 profile assembles the verified career timeline across all systems.
integrations.gaps: No public API confirmed (Q1); 'NSRS ID' vs KID terminology unresolved (Q13).

integrations.why: KIRTI assessments are the highest-signal standardised talent data points for school-age athletes.
integrations.dataFlow: KIRTI assessment results → 360 timeline entries (verified · SAI centre).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Assessment results stop living in a programme silo and start following the athlete.
integrations.gaps: KIRTI-to-NSRS identity linkage not publicly documented.

integrations.why: Aadhaar-backed verification (via DigiLocker) and APAAR linkage are the dedupe anchors — one person, one profile.
integrations.dataFlow: eKYC verification events (yes/no + minimal fields) — raw Aadhaar numbers never stored.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Duplicate and ghost profiles become detectable at registration time.
integrations.gaps: Aadhaar usage rules for a sports platform need legal confirmation.

integrations.why: Selection committees need to know an athlete's doping status — but testing data lives in WADA's ADAMS under strict rules.
integrations.dataFlow: Status flag only (eligible / sanctioned, with sanction window) — never test details.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Selectors stop discovering sanctions from newspapers.
integrations.gaps: NADA/WADA data-sharing constraints are stricter than any other integration here.

integrations.why: Khelo India Games results and TOPS/NCOE participation are core verified timeline events.
integrations.dataFlow: Games results + programme participation → 360 timeline (verified · source programme).
integrations.kbValueAdd: A single career view across programmes that currently do not share screens.
integrations.gaps: Games Management System (GMS) integration path unconfirmed (Q29).

integrations.why: Fit India assessment history gives the 360 profile its grassroots fitness baseline.
integrations.dataFlow: School-age fitness assessments → baseline metrics on the timeline (consent-gated).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Talent signals visible years before formal competition results exist.
integrations.gaps: Fit India app data linkage to a sports profile is not an existing feature.

integrations.why: SAI already operates a Games Management System (GMS) for Khelo India Games — M8 must coordinate with it, not duplicate it.
integrations.dataFlow: Sanctioned-event calendar ↔ GMS; Khelo India Games results → verified results feed.
integrations.kbValueAdd: One national calendar and one results standard across events GMS does not cover (school, district, federation).
integrations.gaps: GMS scope, availability to non-Khelo-India events, and integration path unconfirmed (Q29).

integrations.why: Event entries keyed to the athlete's KID make every start list verifiable and every result attributable.
integrations.dataFlow: Entry validation ← NSRS identity; participation + results → athlete 360 timeline.
integrations.kbValueAdd: No more paper entry forms with unverifiable identities; age fraud gets harder.
integrations.gaps: Depends on the same unconfirmed NSRS API as M7.

integrations.why: Participation and medal certificates issued straight to DigiLocker are forgery-resistant and instantly verifiable.
integrations.dataFlow: KB as DigiLocker issuer → certificates pushed to participants' lockers.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Certificates that scheme applications (M3) and marks-for-sports (M16) can trust without manual verification.
integrations.gaps: Requires issuer onboarding — same SOP as M3's requester role.
